deployedMainSubject: feat(themes-v2): Phase 5.3 PR-2 backend — sections_json reorder + customer_form->confirm_summary rename (#484)

normalizerNote.description: Per-request Sentry baggage tag (trace_id / transaction / sampled / sample_rand) is non-deterministic. Masks the entire <meta name="baggage"> value to [BAGGAGE]. Subsumes the older sentry_baggage_trace_id and sentry_baggage_sample_rand partial-field masks (kept in 07.js for defense-in-depth in case the baggage content is ever inlined elsewhere).
normalizerNote.rootCause: sentry-transaction is emitted only when sentry-sampled=true, and sentry-sampled flips per request (~10% sample rate). Partial-field masks left intermittent presence/absence drift — surfaced as karamhomes 2026-06-02 45-byte hunk on the first verify run.
normalizerNote.verifiedEquivalentClaimAppliesTo: 07.js including the sentry_baggage_meta rule (committed at harnessHeadAtCommit above). Re-running 07.js at any prior SHA against this baseline + a fresh capture will surface a 1-hunk DIFF on whichever tenant draws sentry-sampled=true that run.

verification.approach: Captured a fresh prod re-run using the same captureCommand as this baseline, then diffed against the baseline. The verifier capture itself was discarded after the verdict was recorded (same per-request Sentry IDs and cache-warmth state as any future capture — re-deriving on demand is more honest than committing point-in-time receipts).
verification.reverificationInstructions: Run captureCommand into a fresh --output-dir, then run scripts/themes_v2/07_diff_against_baseline.js --baseline-dir=snapshots/2026-06-02-flag-off --current-dir=<fresh-dir>. Expect 7/7 PASS while deployed code is at deployedMainSha. After PR-3 lands a render change on main, re-running will surface the intended diffs.

notes: Raw bytes in this baseline encode wall-clock capture moment: per-request Sentry trace_ids (masked by sentry_trace_meta and sentry_baggage_meta) and cache-warmth state (snapshotUsed / snapshotVersion, masked by snapshot_used_field and snapshot_version_field). A re-capture into the same dir would replace these bytes without changing the verified-equivalence claim — the equivalence holds at the normalizer's output, not at raw sha256.

The 'protected' flag marks tenants where appearance / branding changes ship through a stricter review (3 = birdie-golf, 21 = alrazi, 33 = karamhomes). Useful as an attention prior when triaging future DIFF verdicts.
